news.title: Why Trump reversed course to fast-track psychedelic drugs for mental healthcare
news.summary: President Donald Trump is trying to fast-track research into psychedelics as treatments. The move could create new opportunities and risks, experts say.

news.title: Defense spending, China in Asia and lessons from Ukraine: Takeways from the 2026 IISS Shangri-La Dialogue
news.summary: Top world leaders, defence officials and key executives will gather in Singapore from May 29 to 31. The summit sees top world leaders and defence officials gathering in Singapore.

news.title: Oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz might not return to levels seen before the Iran war
news.summary: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has seriously challenged freedom of navigation in the sea lane. The blockade has raised questions about the future of the strait.

news.title: Analysis: An end to the Iran war may be just the beginning of a new era of U.S. inequality
news.summary: Stocks have boomed while consumers have paid the costs of high energy prices. Consumers have been hit with higher gas and electricity prices in recent months. Stocks have also boomed as a result.

news.title: In deeply red Oklahoma, minimum wage’s biggest 2026 election cycle fight is about to be decided
news.summary: Oklahomans will soon vote on a measure to double the minimum wage. The issue may reflect the economic mood and inflation outlook across parties.

news.title: Here’s how Gap’s yoga-wear maker Athleta went from must-have to ‘generic’
news.summary: Athleta’s expected recovery has been pushed out for three straight years, as an attempt to widen its appeal ended up “appealing to no one in particular”

news.title: My golf buddy dropped me when I didn’t ask him to be my financial adviser. Was I naive?
news.summary: “Friendliness alone is not a sufficient reason to trust someone with your finances,” she says. “Friendly’s not the same as ‘trust me,’’ she adds.

news.title: My friend, 62, earns $20,000 a year. Should she take Social Security now — and claim survivor’s benefit at 67?
news.summary: “I calculated her break-even point to be around age 78,” he said. “I’m not sure she’s going to be able to do it.”
